evals.description: Trigger test: activates on a security incident report
evals.prompt: We just got an alert that someone logged into our admin panel from an IP in a country we've never seen before and downloaded our entire user database. What do I do right now?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on breach response question
evals.prompt: Our security team thinks we had a breach three weeks ago. We're not sure what was accessed. How do we start a formal incident response?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on ransomware incident
evals.prompt: Files on our file server are being encrypted and a ransom note appeared. What are the first five things we should do?

We received a report from a researcher that a read-only API key was exposed in a public GitHub repo for about 6 hours. The key only has access to non-PII analytics data. How do I classify the severity of this incident?

evals.description: Core task: preserving forensic evidence correctly
evals.prompt: We believe a Linux server was compromised. Before we reimage it, what forensic evidence do I need to collect and in what order?
We have a confirmed P1 breach in progress. Our VP of Engineering is asking for an update to send to the CEO. What should that message contain?

evals.description: Core task: writing an incident report
evals.prompt: Our P2 security incident is resolved. I need to write the formal incident report. What sections should it include and what level of detail is expected?

evals.description: Core task: IOC analysis and MITRE ATT&CK mapping
evals.prompt: During our investigation we found a scheduled task calling out to an unusual domain every 10 minutes, a new local admin account, and several files with randomized names in the Windows temp folder. How do I analyze these IOCs?

evals.description: Core task: conducting a blameless lessons learned
evals.prompt: A developer accidentally committed AWS credentials to a public repo which led to a P2 incident. How should we run the post-mortem without it turning into a blame session?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against rebooting a compromised system immediately
evals.prompt: We found a server that we think has malware on it. Our first instinct was to reboot it to clear the malware. Is that the right call?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against speculating in external communications
evals.prompt: We're not sure how many customer records were accessed in the breach. Our CEO wants to send a customer notification email today saying 'no customer data was compromised.' Should we send it?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: correctly states GDPR notification window and advises legal consultation
evals.prompt: We discovered a breach yesterday that may have exposed EU customer data. When do we need to notify the data protection authority and who should be involved in that decision?
